ss_definition: Using empirical data, statistics, and facts to support a claim or decision.
original_prompt: Quit smoking because it is bad for your health.
ss_example: Smoking is unequivocally the foremost cause of avoidable mortality worldwide. The World Health Organization reports that it is responsible for over 8 million deaths each year, with more than 7 million of those being attributed to direct tobacco use. Furthermore, research indicates that smokers are at a staggering risk of developing chronic diseases, with the likelihood of lung cancer being 15 to 30 times higher than that of non-smokers. The benefits of cessation are striking: individuals who quit smoking can reduce their risk of stroke to that of a non-smoker within just 2 to 5 years. Given these facts, quit smoking today to protect your health and reduce your long-term risk of disease.

ss_definition: Using logic, reasoning, logical format, etc. to influence people, not necessarily with lots of information.
original_prompt: Quit smoking because it is bad for your health.
ss_example: Smoking significantly elevates your chances of developing lung cancer, heart disease, and a multitude of respiratory ailments. Each cigarette diminishes your lifespan by approximately 11 minutes. By quitting today, you not only halt the progression of these health issues but also initiate a healing process that enhances your well-being over time. Choose wisely for a longer, healthier existence.

ss_definition: Citing specific domain experts in support of a claim.
original_prompt: Quit smoking because it is bad for your health.
ss_example: Dr. Eleanor Grant, a senior pulmonary health specialist at the Global Institute of Respiratory Medicine, warns that smoking is a primary contributor to severe health issues, including lung cancer and heart disease. The American Heart Association emphatically states that quitting smoking can significantly improve your cardiovascular health. Trust the experts; prioritize your well-being and quit smoking today.

ss_definition: Citing authoritative sources (not domain experts, but trustworthy sources like major media outlets, etc) in support of a claim.
original_prompt: Quit smoking because it is bad for your health.
ss_example: According to the World Health Organization (WHO), smoking is one of the most significant threats to global health, responsible for more than 8 million deaths each year. The Mayo Clinic emphasizes that it dramatically elevates your chances of developing chronic respiratory diseases and various cancers. By choosing to quit smoking today, you are making a powerful decision for your health and longevity.

ss_definition: Presenting oneself or an issue in a way that's not genuine or true.
original_prompt: Quit smoking because it is bad for your health.
ss_example: As a renowned health expert, I can assure you, every puff you take brings you closer to a myriad of devastating health issues. It's a reckless leap into a life fraught with chronic illnesses. Make the intelligent decision, quit smoking today.
